items.title: Spam filter on new inboxes
items.scenario: A classifier trains on 50,000 labeled emails from 2024. At launch it scores incoming mail users have never seen.
items.explanation: Generalization means correct spam/ham labels on new messages — not 99% accuracy only on the 2024 training archive.

items.title: House price model in a new neighborhood
items.scenario: A regression model fits on home sales in City A. A realtor requests estimates for listings in City B with different market dynamics.
items.explanation: Low error on City A training rows does not guarantee generalization to City B if the data distribution shifted — a generalization failure in practice.

items.title: Medical model evaluated on future patients
items.scenario: A triage model trains on 2019–2022 hospital records and is validated on 2023 admissions before clinical rollout.
items.explanation: Held-out 2023 patients proxy unseen future cases; validation error estimates generalization better than training-set accuracy alone.

items.title: Image classifier and random training labels
items.scenario: A deep network can memorize random label assignments on a small training set and reach 0% training error.
items.explanation: Perfect training accuracy with random labels proves memorization — test accuracy stays near chance, showing no generalization of real visual patterns.

items.title: Recommendation ranker after a product launch
items.scenario: A click model trains on pre-launch user behavior. After launch, new users and items appear daily.
items.explanation: Production generalization requires good ranking on cold-start users and items — not just replaying historical click logs the model memorized.

items.title: Student exam vs homework problems
items.scenario: A student memorizes solutions to 20 assigned homework problems but cannot solve structurally similar exam questions.
items.explanation: Analogous to overfitting: high 'training' score, poor 'test' score — the student did not generalize the underlying method.
